title: HTML Quotations: Displaying Quotes and References

meta.title: HTML Quotations – blockquote, q, cite & abbr (2026)
meta.description: Learn how to use HTML quotation tags like blockquote, q, cite, and abbr with examples and best practices.

content.value: HTML quotation elements are used to define quotes, references, and abbreviations in a structured and semantic way.
The <blockquote> tag is used for long quotations, usually displayed as a block with indentation.
This is a long quotation from another source.
The <q> tag is used for short inline quotations.
The <cite> tag is used to reference the title of a work such as a book, movie, or article.
The <abbr> tag defines abbreviations and shows the full form when hovered.
HTML is used for web pages.
The <address> tag is used for contact information, usually displayed in italics.

Written by John Doe
Visit us at example.com

value: **Use semantic tags instead of styling only**
**Using blockquote for styling instead of quotes**

content.value: HTML quotation elements help structure quotes and references semantically, improving readability and accessibility.

faq.question: What is blockquote used for?
faq.answer: It is used for long quotations.

faq.question: What is the difference between q and blockquote?
faq.answer: q is for inline quotes, blockquote is for block-level quotes.

faq.question: Why use abbr?
faq.answer: It provides full meaning of abbreviations on hover.

quiz.question: Which tag is used for long quotes?
Which tag is used for inline quotes?
Which tag shows abbreviation full form?
